summary: Full backend and frontend testing of new authentication system: national ID login, auto-create user accounts, account states, brute force protection, PIN change. 30/30 backend tests passed. Frontend UI verified. One critical bug found: PIN change modal never shows due to PublicRoute race condition.

ui_bugs.issue: PIN change modal (must_change_pin=true) NEVER shows after login. Root cause: AuthContext.login() calls setUser() making isAuthenticated=true, PublicRoute immediately redirects to '/', LoginPage unmounts before setShowPinChange(true) can render the modal. Employee is logged in without being forced to change PIN.

Password field has maxLength={isNationalId ? 6 : undefined}. When identifier is a national ID, PIN is limited to 6 chars. Employee numbers > 6 chars (e.g. 'EMP-001', 'UI-TEST-001') cannot be entered as default PIN. The stored default PIN (hash(employee_number)) won't match if employee_number > 6 chars. Only affects employees where employee_number is > 6 characters.
ui_bugs.selector: LoginPage.jsx - password Input with maxLength={isNationalId ? 6 : undefined}
ui_bugs.priority: MEDIUM - Only affects employee numbers > 6 chars. Real use case with numeric IDs like '123456' is fine.

features_verified: ✅ Login page shows 'رقم الهوية الوطنية' label with placeholder '1xxxxxxxxx'
✅ Admin can login with email admin@crowd.sa + admin123
✅ National ID format validation: '6 أرقام متبقية' for partial, '✓ رقم هوية صحيح' for valid 10-digit
✅ Password label changes to 'كلمة المرور (PIN)' when national ID detected
✅ Add Employee dialog has national_id field with 10-digit validation
✅ Validation message '✓ سيُنشأ حساب تلقائياً عند الإضافة' appears for valid national ID
✅ New employee with national_id creates account automatically (account_created=true)
✅ New employee account starts as 'pending' status in employee table
✅ Account status column 'الحساب' visible in employee table with status badges
✅ Activate button (✓ تفعيل) visible for pending accounts
✅ After activation, account status badge shows 'نشط' (active)
✅ Freeze (🔒 تجميد) and Reset PIN (🔑) buttons appear after activation
✅ Arabic error messages shown on wrong password: 'كلمة المرور غير صحيحة. متبقي N محاولة قبل القفل'
✅ Brute force protection: 5 wrong attempts lock account (backend confirmed)
✅ Locked account shows Arabic message '...تم قفل الحساب...'
✅ Manager can reset PIN via reset-pin endpoint (backend confirmed)
✅ All account management endpoints return Arabic success/error messages
action_items: HIGH: Fix PIN change modal not showing - in PublicRoute (App.js), add check: if (isAuthenticated && user?.must_change_pin) allow login page to stay (don't redirect). Fix: change PublicRoute condition to: if (isAuthenticated && !user?.must_change_pin) return <Navigate to='/' replace />;

MEDIUM: Password field maxLength={isNationalId ? 6 : undefined} - Consider removing maxLength or increasing to match employee_number max length. Currently limits PIN entry to 6 chars when national ID is in identifier field, breaking login for employees with employee_number > 6 chars.
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL: PublicRoute in App.js redirects to '/' as soon as isAuthenticated=true. LoginPage.handleLogin() calls setShowPinChange(true) AFTER login() resolves, but by then AuthContext has already set user/isAuthenticated=true, causing PublicRoute to navigate away. Fix: PublicRoute should check `isAuthenticated && !user?.must_change_pin` before redirecting.

HIGH: LoginPage.jsx line 257: maxLength={isNationalId ? 6 : undefined} on password field. For national ID users, default PIN = employee_number. If employee_number is > 6 chars, user cannot enter their full PIN. Fix: increase maxLength to at least 20, or remove it entirely for password fields.
NOTE: The PIN change modal code itself is correct (PinChangeModal component, data-testid attributes, validation logic all work). The issue is purely the routing/auth state race condition.

success_rate.frontend: 80% (PIN change modal flow broken, all other UI features work)

seed_data_creation: Created and deleted UI test employee (1122334455 / UI-TEST-001 → 123456). All test employees cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New auth system backend is 100% working. Key UX bug: PIN change modal never shows after login. Root cause: PublicRoute in App.js redirects to '/' when isAuthenticated=true, but the LoginPage's showPinChange state is set AFTER login() resolves. When user has must_change_pin=true, they bypass the mandatory PIN change and go straight to dashboard. Fix needed in PublicRoute check. Also: password field maxLength=6 issue for national ID login. Otherwise all features work: national ID login, auto-create account, pending status, activate/freeze/reset-pin buttons in employee table, account status column all verified working.

rca_of_issue.root_cause: Race condition in auth flow: AuthContext.login() sets setUser() → isAuthenticated=true → PublicRoute renders <Navigate to='/' /> → LoginPage unmounts → setShowPinChange(true) never renders modal
rca_of_issue.reproduction: 1. Create employee with national_id + activate 2. Login with national_id + employee_number as PIN 3. must_change_pin=true in DB but modal never appears, user goes straight to dashboard
rca_of_issue.fix: In App.js PublicRoute: change `if (isAuthenticated) return <Navigate to='/' />` to `if (isAuthenticated && !user?.must_change_pin) return <Navigate to='/' />`. Also expose `user` from useAuth() in PublicRoute.
rca_of_issue.alternative_fix: In AuthContext.login(), don't call setUser() when must_change_pin=true. Instead store a pendingUser state and only set user after PIN change.
